steps.title: Map the passage's structure
steps.content: Sentence 1: focus on motor control has long "hindered recognition of other possible functions" of the cerebellum. Sentence 2: recent research now shows it also regulates emotion and social behavior, plus a pathway to the VTA.

steps.title: State the main idea in one line
steps.content: The cerebellum was traditionally seen as a motor-control structure, but recent neuroscience is uncovering additional roles.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The recent verification of a pathway between the VTA and the cerebellum confirms the cerebellum's long-suspected role in motor coordination.: misread — the new pathway is to the VTA (reward/motivation), not a confirmation of motor coordination. ✗
  • Choice B — Recent advances in the field of neuroscience have challenged widely accepted claims about the function of a pathway connecting the VTA and the cerebellum.: overreach — no prior "widely accepted claim" about that pathway is mentioned; the pathway is a new finding. ✗
  • Choice C — The cerebellum has primarily been thought to regulate motor functioning, but in recent years neuroscience researchers have been uncovering additional functions.: captures both halves of the passage — old view plus new functions. ✓
  • Choice D — Technological limitations have historically hindered the study of the cerebellum, but the recent development of new technologies has led to greater insights into its functions.: fabricated — the text blames the focus on motor control, not technology. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
